name: Alcalá de Henares and Los Santos de la Humosa Loop
name_original: Torrejón de Ardoz, Alcalá de Henares, Meco, Anchuelo, Mejorada del Campo – ruta circular
description: This moderate 53.3-mile road cycling route connects Torrejón de Ardoz, Alcalá de Henares, Meco, Anchuelo, and Mejorada del Campo.
region_intro: Road cycling around Torrejón De Ardoz features varied landscapes with accessible routes and significant green spaces. The terrain includes mostly paved surfaces, making it suitable for road cyclists. The Henares River is a recurring feature, and routes often incorporate views from elevated points like Cerro de El Viso. The area provides a mix of flat sections and moderate ascents. Best road cycling routes around Torrejón De Ardoz The most popular road cycling route is Alcalá de Henares and Los Santos de la Humosa Loop, a 53.3 miles (85.7 km) trail that takes 3 hours 41 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers a long-distance ride through varied local scenery. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Pioz Castle – Corpa loop from Torrejón de Ardoz, a moderate 45.7 miles (73.5 km) path. This route provides scenic views and passes by historical landmarks. Local road cyclists also love the Bar Aries – Café Lito loop from Torrejón de Ardoz, a 35.6 miles (57.3 km) trail leading through local points of interest, often completed in about 2 hours 31 minutes. Road cycling around Torrejón De Ardoz is defined by the Henares River, green spaces, and views from hills like Cerro de El Viso. The network offers options for different ability levels, from moderate to more challenging rides. The routes in Torrejón De Ardoz are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 1,500 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Torrejón De Ardoz's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: There are over 35 road cycling routes around Torrejón De Ardoz, catering to various skill levels. The komoot community has explored more than 1,500 of these routes.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners in Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: Yes, Torrejón De Ardoz offers 8 easy road cycling routes, perfect for beginners or those looking for a more relaxed ride. These routes typically feature less elevation gain and shorter distances.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes around Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: The terrain around Torrejón De Ardoz is varied, featuring mostly paved surfaces ideal for road cycling. You'll encounter a mix of flat sections, moderate ascents, and scenic views, often alongside the Henares River or from elevated points like Cerro de El Viso.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced riders in the area?
A: Absolutely. For experienced riders seeking a challenge, there are 6 difficult road cycling routes around Torrejón De Ardoz. These routes often include significant elevation gains, such as the 51.4 km route with a 540-meter elevation gain that includes the Alto de la Valdelavieja loop.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while road cycling in Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: Many routes incorporate picturesque natural features. You can enjoy views of the Henares River, explore the extensive green spaces of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3896331" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Europa Park&lt;/a&gt;, or take in magnificent vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/332622" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Hill with View in Juan Carlos I Park&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also pass by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6115319" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Cerros Forest Park&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical sites or points of interest along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, several routes offer glimpses of historical and cultural landmarks. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19852950" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pioz Castle – Corpa loop from Torrejón de Ardoz&lt;/a&gt; passes by historical sites. You might also encounter the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2393087" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ruins of Alcalá la Vieja Fortress&lt;/a&gt; or the vibrant &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3931238" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervantes Square&lt;/a&gt; in nearby Alcalá de Henares.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: The road cycling routes in Torrejón De Ardoz are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 30 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied scenery, well-maintained paths, and the blend of natural beauty with accessible routes.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available in Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: Yes, many of the road cycling routes in Torrejón De Ardoz are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977040140/alcala-de-henares-and-los-santos-de-la-humosa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Henares and Los Santos de la Humosa Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31837908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Aries – Café Lito loop from Torrejón de Ardoz&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Can I find longer road cycling trails in the Torrejón De Ardoz area?
A: Certainly. For those looking for longer rides, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e977040140/alcala-de-henares-and-los-santos-de-la-humosa-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alcalá de Henares and Los Santos de la Humosa Loop&lt;/a&gt; span over 85 km, offering extensive distances through varied local scenery. The region is well-connected to nearby towns, allowing for longer excursions.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, some routes are known to pass by local establishments where you can take a break. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31837908" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bar Aries – Café Lito loop from Torrejón de Ardoz&lt;/a&gt; specifically highlights local points of interest, including cafes, making it convenient for refreshment stops.

Q: What is the best season for road biking in Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road biking in Torrejón De Ardoz, with milder temperatures and less extreme weather. Summer can be quite hot, especially midday, while winter can bring cooler temperatures, though many days remain suitable for cycling.

Q: Are there scenic viewpoints on the road cycling routes around Torrejón De Ardoz?
A: Yes, the region is known for its scenic vistas. Routes often incorporate magnificent views from elevated points like Cerro de El Viso. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39566116" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoints – Plaza del Pueblo de Loeches loop from Torrejón de Ardoz&lt;/a&gt; is specifically designed to highlight these picturesque spots.
tips: The Romans founded a town they called Complutum, about 2 km from the current Alcalá de Henares, which was one of the most important Roman settlements on the Iberian Peninsula. After 400 years under Roman rule, the Moors arrived and built the castle of A-Qalát de Nahar in 850, from which the city got its name. Alcalá de Henares is also the birthplace of Miguel de Cervantes, author of the world famous “Don Quixote”, and you can visit the house where he was born, which is now a museum.

It is presumably the work of the late fifteenth or early sixteenth. In its restored interior, a modern altarpiece appears in the background, with the Archangel in the center, and on its sides some paintings by Carmen Vives Camino.
It is worth traveling those kilometers to see the views offered by this large and spacious viewpoint. You can see from Meco to the 4 towers located in Madrid.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: This road cycling route is approximately 85.7 km long and generally takes around 3 hours and 41 minutes to complete, depending on your pace and stops.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on this route?
faqs.answer: The terrain along this route is characterized by rolling hills and flat sections, rather than significant mountainous climbs. You'll encounter mostly paved surfaces suitable for road bikes, with gentle inclines and descents, particularly around areas like Alcalá de Henares and Meco.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the way?
faqs.answer: The route offers a blend of urban, historic, and agricultural landscapes. Key highlights include the Historic Centre of Alcalá de Henares, a UNESCO World Heritage site, the Parish Church of San Miguel, and the Viewpoint of Los Santos de la Humosa, offering panoramic views.

faqs.question: Is this road cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Given its length and rolling terrain, this route is classified as moderate difficulty. While it doesn't feature major mountain passes, the significant distance and some individual climbs mean it's best suited for cyclists with a reasonable level of fitness rather than absolute beginners.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October) when temperatures are milder. Summers can be very hot in the Community of Madrid, and winters can be chilly, though cycling is possible year-round with appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Where can I park my car to start this route?
faqs.answer: As this is a circular route connecting several towns, you have flexibility for parking. You could look for public parking in the town centers of Torrejón de Ardoz, Alcalá de Henares, or Mejorada del Campo. Ensure to check local parking regulations and availability, especially in urban areas.

faqs.question: Do I need any special permits or pay entrance fees to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: No, you do not need any special permits or have to pay entrance fees to cycle this route. It primarily uses public roads and paths, so it's freely accessible.

faqs.question: How can I access the route using public transport with my bike?
faqs.answer: Many of the towns along the route, such as Torrejón de Ardoz and Alcalá de Henares, are well-connected by regional trains (Cercanías) and buses from Madrid. Check the specific public transport operator's rules regarding bicycle carriage, as policies can vary by time of day and line.

faqs.question: Are there places to stop for food or drinks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, as the route passes through several towns including Torrejón de Ardoz, Alcalá de Henares, Meco, Anchuelo, and Mejorada del Campo, you'll find numerous opportunities for refreshments. These towns offer various cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can refuel.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for cycling with children or families?
faqs.answer: Due to its significant distance (over 85 km) and moderate difficulty, this route is generally not recommended for young children or casual family cycling. It's better suited for experienced road cyclists. For family-friendly options, consider shorter, dedicated cycling paths within individual municipalities.

faqs.question: Is this route suitable for cycling with pets?
faqs.answer: This is a road cycling route primarily on public roads. While pets are generally allowed in public spaces, cycling with a pet on a leash on busy roads can be unsafe for both the cyclist and the animal. It's best to consider dedicated bike paths or parks if you wish to cycle with your pet.
